examples.english: I never really clicked with my boss, and it made work a little more difficult.
examples.chinese: 我和老板一直合不来，这给工作带来了一些困难。

examples.english: The two women struck up a friendship when they met on holiday.
examples.chinese: 这两位女子在度假时遇上，就做起朋友来。

examples.english: We met for the first time at a business conference, and we immediately struck up a friendship which has lasted for years.
examples.chinese: 我们在一次商务会议上认识之后便马上建立了友谊，我们的友谊已持续了许多年。

examples.english: His parents befriended some American soldiers who served in Wales during World War II.
examples.chinese: 他的父母与一些第二次世界大战期间在威尔士服役的美国兵成了朋友。

examples.english: It's fairly unusual for high school seniors to befriend freshmen.
examples.chinese: 高中的毕业班学生与一年级学生交上朋友是很不寻常的事。

expressions.text: fall in with/ get in with

examples.english: He's fallen in with a group of boys I don't like very much.
examples.chinese: 他与一帮我不大喜欢的男孩交上了朋友。

examples.english: She used her husband's money and family to get in with a group of Hollywood's social elite.
examples.chinese: 她利用丈夫的财富和家庭关系结交了一群好莱坞社交精英。

centers.english: a friendly relationship with someone

examples.english: Our marriage is based on friendship, love, and trust.
examples.chinese: 我们的婚姻是以友谊、爱情和信任为基础的。

examples.english: We didn't see each other much during that time, but our friendship remained strong.
examples.chinese: 那个时候我俩难得见面，可是我们的友情仍然牢固。

examples.english: I had no close friendships with other boys at school.
examples.chinese: 我读书时跟其他男孩没有什么要好的交情。

examples.english: Bernstein's visit to Copland's studio led to a friendship between the two composers.
examples.chinese: 伯恩斯坦去科普兰的工作室拜访他，使这两位作曲家建立起了友谊。

examples.english: He was lonely and looking for companionship.
examples.chinese: 他很孤独，在寻找友谊。

examples.english: Older people often benefit from having a pet for companionship.
examples.chinese: 老年人常因有宠物作伴而获益。

centers.english: to have important or rich friends who can help you
centers.chinese: 有地位高的或富有的朋友帮助

examples.english: He won't lose his job -- he has plenty of friends in high places.
examples.chinese: 他不会丢掉工作的一他有许多身居高位的朋友。

examples.english: The couple were well-educated and well-connected members of an elite social class.
examples.chinese: 这对夫妇受过良好教育，属于有上层社会关系的精英阶层。

examples.english: Richardson is very well-connected, both in the Democratic leadership and on political committees.
examples.chinese: 理查森在民主党领导层内以及在政治委员会中都有很好的关系。

centers.english: to stop being friendly with someone

examples.english: It was the first time Bill and I had fallen out.
examples.chinese: 这是比尔和我第一次闹翻。

examples.english: She fell out with some of her school friends.
examples.chinese: 她和几个要好的同学闹翻了。

examples.english: I didn't think it was worth falling out over, but Emily obviously did.
examples.chinese: 我觉得不值得为这件事吵翻，但埃米莉显然认为值得。

centers.english: someone you are fighting against, especially in a war
centers.chinese: 你在对抗的人，尤指在战争中

examples.english: You cannot attack an enemy unless you have precise information about their numbers and position.
examples.chinese: 如果没有掌握敌军人数和位置方面的准确情况，就不能发动进攻。

examples.english: They accused him of giving secret information to the enemy.
examples.chinese: 他们指控他向敌军泄露机密情报。

examples.english: Britain and France decided to unite and fight against their common enemy.
examples.chinese: 英法决定联合起来对付共同的敌人。

examples.english: One man was ordered to observe enemy aircraft and to warn when danger was imminent.
examples.chinese: 一名士兵被派观察敌机动向，在出现危险情况时发出警告。

examples.english: Mitterrand drew France closer to the European union and to Germany, its former foe.
examples.chinese: 密特朗拉近了法国与欧盟以及它过去的敌人——德国之间的距离。

examples.english: As we approached the camp a guard called out: ‘Who goes there -- friend or foe?’
examples.chinese: 我们走近军营时，一个警卫喊道：“谁在那里—是朋友还是敌人？”

examples.english: The peace talks proved that even great adversaries were capable of cooperation.
examples.chinese: 这次和谈证明，即使是冤家对头也能合作。

examples.english: Symes grabbed his adversary by the throat and wrestled him to the ground.
examples.chinese: 赛姆斯掐住对手的喉咙，将他摔倒在地上。

examples.english: Hostile forces have taken control of cities in the north of the country.
examples.chinese: 敌军已占领了该国北部的几座城市。

examples.english: The ships had travelled thousands of miles through hostile waters to converge in the Atlantic.
examples.chinese: 舰队穿过敌方水域行驶了几千英里来到大西洋会师。

centers.english: someone who hates you and wants to harm you
centers.chinese: 仇恨你并且想伤害你的人

examples.english: Did your husband have any enemies?
examples.chinese: 你的丈夫有什么仇敌吗？

examples.english: My parents sometimes seem to treat me as if I was their enemy.
examples.chinese: 我父母对我有时候就像对冤家似的。

centers.english: behaving in a friendly way

examples.english: Ella was very friendly, and I liked her immediately.
examples.chinese: 埃拉这个人很友好，一见面我就喜欢上她了。

examples.english: The hotel staff were very friendly and helpful.
examples.chinese: 这家旅馆的员工非常友好，乐于助人。

examples.english: One surprise was how friendly everyone was to us on our travels.
examples.chinese: 在旅行途中人人都对我们很友好，这是让我们感到意外的一件事。

examples.english: Everywhere he was treated with kindness and friendliness.
examples.chinese: 他在各个地方都受到亲切友好的接待。

examples.english: We met some really nice people at the party - friends of my sister.
examples.chinese: 我在聚会上认识了我姐姐的一些朋友，他们都非常友好。

examples.english: All Brad's friends were very nice to me, but I was too shy to join in their chat.
examples.chinese: 布拉德的朋友都对我非常友好，但我太害羞，未能加入他们的聊天。

expressions.text: easy to get on with /easy to get along wit

examples.english: What's he like? Is he easy to get on with?
examples.chinese: 他这人怎么样？好相处吗?

examples.english: As a director, I'm always looking for good actors, but it's a bonus if they are easy to get along with too.
examples.chinese: 作为导演我总是在寻觅优秀的演员，只是如果他们也性情随和就更好了。

examples.english: I feel lucky to have such an easygoing and affectionate child.
examples.chinese: 有这么性格随和、态度亲切的孩子我感到幸运。

examples.english: Stein's easygoing manner is only one of the reasons he will be missed when he retires.
examples.chinese: 斯坦那随和的举止不过是他退休后大家会思念他的原因之

examples.english: She's a warm, caring person, and she'll make a wonderful nurse.
examples.chinese: 她为人热情又关心人，会成为一个很好的护士。

examples.english: He welcomed us with a warm smile.
examples.chinese: 他热情地微笑着迎接我们。

examples.english: The Secretary General was warmly welcomed at the White House yesterday.
examples.chinese: 秘书长昨天在白宫受到了热情的欢迎。

examples.english: If you have any problems, the head teacher is very approachable
examples.chinese: 如果你有什么问题的话可找校长，校长很平易近人。

examples.english: Olivia has a reputation as an easygoing, approachable executive who always has lunch with her employees.
examples.chinese: 奥利维娅是个人所共知的随和而易于接近的经理，她总是与员工一起吃午餐。

examples.english: He stood at the door with a welcoming smile.
examples.chinese: 他站在门口，微笑着表示欢迎。

examples.english: Restaurant and hotel prices in the area are reasonable, and the townspeople are welcoming.
examples.chinese: 该地区的饭店和旅馆价格公道，镇上的居民很热情好客。

examples.english: Most of the people I met in Laos were very hospitable and kind.
examples.chinese: 我在老挝遇到的人大多很好客友善。

examples.english: He was known for his hospitality and generosity.
examples.chinese: 大家都知道他好客大方。

examples.english: She married an affable, middle-aged businessman.
examples.chinese: 她嫁了一位亲切友善的中年商人。

examples.english: Brown was affable and sympathetic, but also firm and decisive in dealing with the problems presented to him.
examples.chinese: 布朗亲切友好而富于同情心，但遇到问题需要他处理时则又坚决果断。

examples.english: The waiter was an amiable young man.
examples.chinese: 侍者是位和蔼的年轻人。

examples.english: Cohen is soft-spoken and amiable.
examples.chinese: 科恩说话轻声柔和，态度和蔼可亲。

examples.english: Dr Saito has a warm, genial manner.
examples.chinese: 斋藤博士态度热情、亲切。

examples.english: Our hosts were genial and friendly, and our stay was a very pleasant one.
examples.chinese: 主人亲切友好，我们在逗留期间住得非常愉快。

centers.english: usually enjoying talking to people and being with them
centers.chinese: 通常喜欢与人交谈并与人在一起

examples.english: She's a friendly, sociable woman.
examples.chinese: 她是个友好、爱与人交往的女人。

examples.english: Some research has shown that people without brothers and sisters tend to be less sociable.
examples.chinese: 有些研究结果显示，没有兄弟姐妹的人一般较不喜欢与人交往。

examples.english: She's become more outgoing since she went to college.
examples.chinese: 她上大学之后变得大方多了。

examples.english: Marshall's skills and her outgoing personality made her very effective in her public relations jobs.
examples.chinese: 马歇尔的交际技巧加上她外向的性格使她在公共关系工作中非常得心应手。

examples.english: Lisa is very extrovert, but her sister is a little shy.
examples.chinese: 莉萨性格很外向，可她妹妹有点腼腆。

examples.english: Brass players have a reputation as the most extroverted musicians - they're the party animals of the orchestra.
examples.chinese: 铜管乐师以性格最为外向的音乐家而出名，他们是乐队里最爱参加聚会的一群人。

examples.english: The work in sales appeals to the extrovert in me.
examples.chinese: 销售工作激发了我性格中外向的一面。

centers.english: friendly relationships/conversations

examples.english: Friendly relations between the two countries have continued through this difficult time.
examples.chinese: 两国之间的友好关系在此困难的时候得以延续。

examples.english: Fontaine said that he was leaving the company on very friendly terms.
examples.chinese: 方丹说他将以十分友好的方式离开公司。

examples.english: The meeting between the two leaders was very amicable.
examples.chinese: 双方领导人的会议气氛很友好。

examples.english: Simons sent his attorney to meet with the ranchers in hopes that they could still come to an amicable settlement.
examples.chinese: 西蒙斯派律师去见牧场主，期望他们仍能以友好的方式达成协议。

examples.english: Britain and Portugal have had cordial relations for more than four centuries.
examples.chinese: 四个多世纪以来英国和葡萄牙一直保持着友好的关系。

examples.english: Donnely was polite and cordial, but she refused to sign the contract.
examples.chinese: 唐纳利态度礼貌温和，但她拒绝在合同上签字。

examples.english: He greeted them cordially.
examples.chinese: 他以亲切的态度与他们打招呼。

centers.english: friendly places/situations

examples.english: The restaurant had good food and a friendly atmosphere.
examples.chinese: 这家餐馆食物精美，气氛友好。

examples.english: You're lucky to work in such a friendly office.
examples.chinese: 在这样一个气氛友好的办公室里工作你真是有福气。

examples.english: A bunch of fresh flowers on the table always looks welcoming.
examples.chinese: 在桌上放一束鲜花看上去总让人感到亲切惬意。

examples.english: a bright, clean, welcoming room
examples.chinese: 一个明亮清洁、令人感到舒适的房间

examples.english: The mood was relaxed and convivial.
examples.chinese: 气氛轻松而友好。

examples.english: Pubs are good places for a drink and some convivial conversation.
examples.chinese: 小酒馆是喝酒谈笑的好地方。

centers.english: too friendly in a way that is unpleasant
centers.chinese: 太友好以致让人觉得不快

examples.english: I don't like it when men I've just met are too familiar.
examples.chinese: 我不喜欢有些男人刚见面就很随便。

examples.english: She came up to me and started talking in such a familiar way that I thought I must have met her before.
examples.chinese: 她走过来用非常随便的态度跟我说起话来，使我以为自己以前肯定见过她。

examples.english: He's been criticized for his smarmy behavior when interviewing celebrities.
examples.chinese: 他遭到批评说他在采访名流时媚态十足。

examples.english: a slick, smarmy public relations officer
examples.chinese: 一位世故圆滑而谄词令色的公关官员

examples.english: He denies calling the Prime Minister ‘smarmy’.
examples.chinese: 他否认自己说过首相是“假殷勤的”。

examples.english: A man in the pub was a little over-friendly, so I left early.
examples.chinese: 小酒馆里有个男人太过友好，于是我提前离开了。

examples.english: The waiter was polite and efficient, but not obsequious.
examples.chinese: 这侍者彬彬有礼，工作勤快，但并不阿谀逢迎。

examples.english: All this obsequious praise for his actions is enough to make most normal people sick.
examples.chinese: 所有这些对他的行为的阿谀逢迎足以使大多数正常人感到恶心。

examples.english: What a slimy, horrible man.
examples.chinese: 多虚伪，多可怕的人啊。

examples.english: They had the usual slimy politician on TV talking about ‘the innate good sense of the voters’.
examples.chinese: 他们照常让那个虚伪的政客在电视上大谈什么“选民天生的明智”。

centers.english: to be too friendly to someone in authority

expressions.text: suck up to somebody/ creep up to somebody/kiss up to somebody

examples.english: Sucking up to the teacher doesn't mean you'll pass your exams.
examples.chinese: 拍老师马屁并不能保证你会通过考试。

examples.english: I'm not going to kiss up to anyone for favors.
examples.chinese: 我不会为了得到好处而去讨好任何人。

examples.english: He was creeping up to the interviewer, trying to look good.
examples.chinese: 他想讨好面试的主考人，争取到好的印象。

examples.english: If a police officer stops your car, be respectful to him, but don't grovel.
examples.chinese: 如果有警察把你的车拦下来，对他要有礼貌，但别奴颜婢膝。

examples.english: The department is having to grovel for money again.
examples.chinese: 该部门又得唯唯诺诺地申请经费了。

examples.english: I grovelled to my parents and promised I wouldn't do it again.
examples.chinese: 我谦卑地向父母保证以后不再干这种事了。

examples.english: He received a grovelling apology.
examples.chinese: 他得到了卑躬屈膝的道歉。

examples.english: Kids don't want other kids to think they're brown-nosing, so they don't tell teachers when they've enjoyed a class.
examples.chinese: 小孩子不愿让其他小孩认为自己在拍马屁，所以当他们喜欢某堂课时不会对老师这么说。

examples.english: She's such a creep at work.
examples.chinese: 她在工作时真是个马屁精。

examples.english: Will's the class creep, and the teachers don't notice.
examples.chinese: 威尔是班上的马屁精，但老师们并未注意到。

centers.english: not friendly

examples.english: It's very difficult to work with Lindsay - she's so unfriendly.
examples.chinese: 与林赛共事很难—她这个人很不友好。

examples.english: I'm sorry if I sounded unfriendly on the phone - I was just tired.
examples.chinese: 要是我在电话里听上去不友善的话，很对不起—我只是累了。

examples.english: The service at the hotel was bad and the staff weren't very friendly.
examples.chinese: 这家酒店服务差，工作人员态度很不友好。

examples.english: The other girls weren't openly unfriendly towards her, but they never invited her along with them.
examples.chinese: 其他女孩子并没有公然对她不友善，但是她们从来不邀请她和她们一起。

examples.english: The fact that he didn't invite you had more to do with insecurity than unfriendliness.
examples.chinese: 他没有邀请你主要是因为自信心不足，而不是他不友好。

examples.english: His manner all evening was cold and unfriendly.
examples.chinese: 整个晚上他都态度冷淡且不友好。

examples.english: Next time she saw Harry he wasn't rude to her, just very cold.
examples.chinese: 她再次见到哈里的时候，他没有对她不礼貌，只是很冷淡。

examples.english: She was oddly cold to him, and I wondered what had happened.
examples.chinese: 她对他出奇地冷淡，我怀疑是不是发生了什么事。

examples.english: He looked at me coldly, but said nothing.
examples.chinese: 他冷冷地看了我一眼，没说一句话。

examples.english: Generally, the people I met in the city were rude and inhospitable.
examples.chinese: 总的来说，我在城里遇到的人都无礼且不热情。

examples.english: So many tourists had visited the monastery that the monks had grown somewhat inhospitable.
examples.chinese: 参观这个修道院的游客众多，修道士渐渐冷淡起来。

examples.english: There was a crowd of hostile demonstrators waiting outside her door.
examples.chinese: 她家门外聚集了一群充满敌意的示威者。

examples.english: He was hostile towards me when I arrived, and the situation did not improve over the next few days.
examples.chinese: 我刚到的时候他就对我有敌意，接下来的几天情况一直如此。

examples.english: Several of the neighbors had become openly hostile to one another.
examples.chinese: 一些邻居之间变得公然互相对抗。

examples.english: I thought I detected a little hostility in his voice.
examples.chinese: 我觉得我听出他的语气里有一丝敌意。

examples.english: Her cool politeness had given way to open hostility.
examples.chinese: 她冷冰冰的客气变成了公开的敌意。

examples.english: I can't understand why he's being so antagonistic.
examples.chinese: 我不明白他为什么有这么大的敌意。

examples.english: Why are Kate and John so antagonistic towards each other?
examples.chinese: 凯特和约翰之间怎么会有这么深的矛盾？

examples.english: The antagonism between her two grown sons was almost too much for Celia to bear.
examples.chinese: 两个成年儿子之间的对立几乎让西莉亚无法承受。

examples.english: The judge's antagonism towards the defendant was clear to everyone.
examples.chinese: 人人都知道法官对被告怀有敌意。

examples.english: After I got the promotion, a few of my co-workers started giving me the cold shoulder.
examples.chinese: 我晋升之后，有一些同事开始冷落我。

examples.english: Everyone at the school was so cliquey, it was hard for me to make new friends.
examples.chinese: 学校里每个人都很排外，我很难交到新朋友。

examples.english: It was a cliquish group, with the older members staying aloof from the younger ones.
examples.chinese: 这是一个派系团体，年纪较大的会员不和较年轻的会员往来。

centers.english: when someone prefers not to be with other people
centers.chinese: 某人不愿与其他人在一起

examples.english: The neighbors seem very distant, although I try to be friendly.
examples.chinese: 尽管我极力表现得友好，邻居似乎很冷淡。

examples.english: As she was growing up, her father was always distant and took little interest in her achievements.
examples.chinese: 她从小父亲对她就很冷淡，对她的成就也漠不关心。

examples.english: Barbara remained aloof behind the barrier of her menu.
examples.chinese: 芭芭拉用菜单挡着自己一直不说话。

examples.english: The organization is controlled by aloof intellectuals who do not take an interest in the ordinary members.
examples.chinese: 该组织是由一群孤傲的知识分子掌控的，他们并不关心普通会员。

examples.english: Sam had a certain aloofness that made people afraid to approach him.
examples.chinese: 萨姆有点孤傲，令人不敢接近。

examples.english: She can be quite stand-offish sometimes.
examples.chinese: 她有时会挺冷漠的。

examples.english: He was well-liked by the senior members of the firm, even though his colleagues found him standoffish and arrogant.
examples.chinese: 他很受公司高层的欢迎，尽管他的同事觉得他冷漠傲慢。

examples.english: Not everyone who likes playing computer games is an antisocial loner.
examples.chinese: 并不是所有喜欢玩电脑游戏的人都性格孤僻。

examples.english: Because she was so shy, people often thought she was antisocial.
examples.chinese: 她很腼腆，所以人们常常觉得她不合群。

examples.english: He kept his arms crossed in front of him and seemed somewhat unapproachable.
examples.chinese: 他双臂交叉抱在胸前，看来有点不可接近。

examples.english: A lot of the guys thought she was attractive, but she seemed so unapproachable that no one dared to talk to her.
examples.chinese: 很多男人都觉得她漂亮，可是她好像很难接近，没有人敢和她说话。

centers.english: when relationships are unfriendly

examples.english: She used to get on well with her neighbours but now things aren't very friendly.
examples.chinese: 她过去和邻里相处得不错，可现在不是很和睦了。

examples.english: Unfriendly relations between the two countries nearly led to war.
examples.chinese: 两国之间的不和差一点导致战争。

expressions.text: on unfriendly terms/not on friendly terms

examples.english: They've been on unfriendly terms ever since their argument.
examples.chinese: 他们自从吵了架之后关系一直不和。

examples.english: The cool relationship between the two men affected the entire team.
examples.chinese: 这两个人之间冷淡的关系影响了全队。

examples.english: When Bill finally arrived, nearly an hour late, he got a rather cool reception.
examples.chinese: 比尔终于赶到时差不多迟了一小时，受到了相当冷淡的接待。

examples.english: The meeting took place in a strained atmosphere.
examples.chinese: 会议在紧张的气氛中举行。

examples.english: Our relationship became very strained because I had refused to lend him money.
examples.chinese: 我们之间的关系因为我拒绝借钱给他而变得十分紧张。

examples.english: After their quarrel, things between Maria and her father were rather frosty.
examples.chinese: 吵了一架之后，玛丽亚和父亲之间的关系颇为冷淡。

examples.english: After five years their marriage turned sour and ended in divorce.
examples.chinese: 五年之后，他们的婚姻变糟，最后离婚了。

examples.english: When the company began to lose money, things turned sour between the directors.
examples.chinese: 公司开始出现亏损之后，董事之间的关系开始变坏。

centers.english: when a particular place or situation seems unfriendly
centers.chinese: 某个地方或情形似乎不友好

examples.english: I don't like going to that bar - it's not very friendly.
examples.chinese: 我不喜欢去那间酒吧—那里气氛不是很友好。

examples.english: The city has a reputation for being unfriendly to minorities.
examples.chinese: 这个城市出了名对少数民族不友好。

examples.english: The entrance to the factory is cold, bare, and unwelcoming.
examples.chinese: 工厂的入口处冷冰冰、光秃秃的，给人一种不受欢迎的感觉。

examples.english: The new fence is just one example of the city's attempt to make public spaces unwelcoming to the homeless.
examples.chinese: 该市企图把街头流浪者拒诸公共场所外，新建的栅栏只是其中一例。

examples.english: The tribe has lived for generations in the inhospitable mountain regions of the country.
examples.chinese: 这个部落世世代代居住在该国荒凉的山区。

examples.english: The brokerage firm has always been extremely inhospitable to women.
examples.chinese: 经纪业一直很排挤女性。

examples.english: They just handed over the keys and walked out - it was all so impersonal.
examples.chinese: 他们交过钥匙就出去了—没有一点人情味。

examples.english: I had no desire to work for a large, impersonal organization.
examples.chinese: 我不想为缺乏人情味的大公司工作。

examples.english: The school was a large, rather forbidding building.
examples.chinese: 那学校是一幢令人相当害怕的大楼。

examples.english: The wood-panelled den looked dark and forbidding.
examples.chinese: 那镶木板的小书房看上去幽暗可怖。

centers.english: when there are no other people with you

examples.english: She was sitting alone on a park bench.
examples.chinese: 她一个人坐在公园的长凳上。

examples.english: I don't really like walking home on my own at night.
examples.chinese: 我不大喜欢夜里一个人走回家。

examples.english: Do you share the apartment, or do you live by yourself?
examples.chinese: 你和别人合住这个套房，还是自己一个人住？

examples.english: Wendy was frightened, all alone in that big old house.
examples.chinese: 温迪孤身一人在那古老的大房子里，感到很害怕。

expressions.text: leave somebody alone/on their own/by themselves

examples.english: The first time his parents left him alone in the house, he set fire to the kitchen.
examples.chinese: 父母第一次把他单独留在家里的时候，他放火把厨房烧起来了。

examples.english: Mark's not well. I can't go out and leave him on his own.
examples.chinese: 马克身体不好，我不能出去，把他一个人留在家里。

examples.english: Children flying unaccompanied are looked after by the cabin crew.
examples.chinese: 无人陪同乘飞机的小孩由乘务人员照顾。

examples.english: Some parts of town are not safe for an unaccompanied woman.
examples.chinese: 镇上的有些地方妇女单独出行不安全。

examples.english: I need solitude in order to paint my pictures.
examples.chinese: 我需要独处来画我的画。

examples.english: Ella loved the quiet solitude of her weekends.
examples.chinese: 埃拉喜欢周末一个人时的那种清静。

examples.english: He spent his free time in solitude, reading or walking in the hills.
examples.chinese: 他空闲时独自一人看看书，或者在山间散散步。

centers.english: when you do something without help from someone else
centers.chinese: 做某事时没有别人的帮助

examples.english: I managed to fix the car on my own.
examples.chinese: 我设法自己修好了汽车。

examples.english: He's old enough to get dressed by himself, isn't he?
examples.chinese: 他到了可以自己穿衣服的年龄了，是吗？

expressions.text: all on your own/all by yourself

examples.english: How did you manage to prepare so much food all by yourself?
examples.chinese: 你一个人是怎么做出这么多食物来的?

examples.english: I can get an application for you, but after that you're on your own.
examples.chinese: 我可以给你弄一张申请表，但以后的事你自己去做。

examples.english: After his illness he was unable to walk unaided.
examples.chinese: 病后他不能独力行走。

examples.english: With no one else in the office I had to deal with the problem unaided.
examples.chinese: 办公室里没有别人，我只得独自处理这个问题。

examples.english: In 1992, he rowed across the Atlantic single-handed.
examples.chinese: 1992年，他一个人划船横渡大西洋。

examples.english: Sanger almost single-handedly founded the birth control movement in the early 1900s.
examples.chinese: 20世纪初，桑格夫人几乎是单枪匹马地发起了节育运动。

examples.english: By the end of the course, all students will fly solo.
examples.chinese: 课程结束的时候，所有学生都要单独飞行。

examples.english: John played with the band for five years before going solo.
examples.chinese: 约翰和乐队一起合作了五年后单飞。

examples.english: Albert wants to take a solo sailing trip around the world.
examples.chinese: 艾伯特想作一次单独航行环游世界。

examples.english: C.J. Walker became one of black America's first self-made millionaires.
examples.chinese: C.J.沃克尔成了美国黑人社会中最早白手起家的百万富翁之一。

examples.english: We're looking for creative self-starters with at least three years’ experience.
examples.chinese: 我们想招聘一些工作积极主动、有创造力的人，他们至少要有三年的工作经验。

centers.english: someone who spends a lot of time alone

examples.english: She was a very solitary woman who didn't make friends easily.
examples.chinese: 她是个喜欢独来独往的人，不轻易交朋友。

examples.english: Ed enjoys the solitary life of a rancher.
examples.chinese: 埃德喜欢牧场上的独居生活。

examples.english: I had always been a loner, and I hated sharing an apartment when I went to college.
examples.chinese: 我一向喜欢独处，所以上大学时讨厌与人合住一套公寓。

examples.english: Joe is one of our best workers but he's too much of a loner to be a good leader.
examples.chinese: 乔是我们这儿工作最出色的人之一，但他太孤僻了，当不了好领导。

examples.english: Old Mr Grimes was a bad-tempered recluse, rarely seen in the town.
examples.chinese: 老格兰姆斯先生脾气古怪，离群索居，在镇上很少见到他。

examples.english: If you don't get out more, you're going to turn into a recluse.
examples.chinese: 如果你不出来多走走，都快成隐士了。

examples.english: We asked him to come and eat with us, but he said he preferred his own company.
examples.chinese: 我们叫他过来一块吃饭，但他说他更喜欢一个人。

centers.english: alone and unhappy

examples.english: Tammy felt very lonely when she first arrived in New York.
examples.chinese: 塔米初到纽约时感到非常寂寞。

examples.english: Our neighbor George is a very lonely man.
examples.chinese: 我们的邻居乔治是个非常孤独的人。

examples.english: I get so lonesome here with no-one to talk to.
examples.chinese: 我在这里连说说话的人都没有，非常寂寞。

examples.english: Many old people complain of loneliness.
examples.chinese: 许多老人都抱怨说觉得寂寞。

examples.english: Young, single parents often feel isolated and unhappy.
examples.chinese: 年轻的单身父母亲常常感到孤独和不快。

examples.english: Children of very rich parents can grow up isolated from the rest of society.
examples.chinese: 父母非常有钱的孩子在成长过程中可能与外界隔绝。

examples.english: At first I couldn't stand the isolation of living in a foreign country.
examples.chinese: 起初，我在异国他乡生活，寂寞难耐。

examples.english: We're making a special effort to help alienated members feel more part of the group.
examples.chinese: 我们正在作特别的努力，帮助感觉受到冷落的人融入这个群体。

examples.english: In high school she felt somehow different and alienated from other students.
examples.chinese: 中学的时候，她总觉得自己与别人不同，和别的同学格格不入。

examples.english: Ray spoke of his daughter's growing alienation from the Church.
examples.chinese: 雷说他女儿和教会的隔阂越来越深了。

centers.english: to feel lonely because someone that you love is not there
centers.chinese: 因所爱的人不在身边而感到寂寞

examples.english: When are you coming home? I miss you.
examples.chinese: 你何时回家？我很想你。

examples.english: It was great living in Prague, but I really missed all my friends.
examples.chinese: 在布拉格的生活很好，但是我非常想念我的所有朋友。

examples.english: ‘What's wrong with Dan?’ ‘I think he's pining for his girlfriend.’
examples.chinese: “丹怎么啦？”“我想他是在想他女朋友吧。”

centers.english: to be unable to find someone or something

examples.english: If you lose your credit card, phone this number immediately.
examples.chinese: 如果丢失信用卡了，请立即拨打这个电话。

examples.english: ‘What are you looking for?’ ‘My purse. I think I might have lost it.’
examples.chinese: “你在找什么？”“我的钱包，我可能把它弄丢了。”

examples.english: Neil put the certificate in a drawer so he wouldn't lose it.
examples.chinese: 尼尔把证件放入抽屉里，这样就不会弄丢。

examples.english: She searched her pockets, but she couldn't find the tickets.
examples.chinese: 她翻遍了所有的口袋，也没找到票。

examples.english: What's happened to Eric? I can't find him anywhere.
examples.chinese: 埃里克怎么了？我在哪也找不到他。

examples.english: I seem to have temporarily mislaid my keys. Have you seen them anywhere?
examples.chinese: 我一下子想不起把钥匙放哪儿了。你看见过吗?

examples.english: If your bank card is lost, mislaid or stolen, call our Card Hotline number.
examples.chinese: 如果你的银行卡丢失、找不到或者被窃，请拨打我们的信用卡热线电话。

examples.english: The company cannot accept liability for loss or damage to a passenger's property.
examples.chinese: 乘客财物若有损失，本公司概不负责。

examples.english: You should report the loss of your passport to the consulate.
examples.chinese: 你遗失护照应该向领事馆报失。

centers.english: someone or something that you cannot find

examples.english: She's been missing for three days now, and we're very worried.
examples.chinese: 到现在她已失踪三天了，我们非常担心。

examples.english: The painting, which has been missing for almost half a century, only turned up when the owner of the house died.
examples.chinese: 这幅遗失了近半个世纪的画作，直到房主去世才又再重现。

examples.english: Police are ‘very concerned’ for the safety of a teenager who's been missing from home for three days.
examples.chinese: 一名十几岁的儿童失踪三天没有回家，警方对他的安全“非常关切”。

examples.english: Two pages were missing from my copy of the report.
examples.chinese: 我的这份报告有两页缺失。

examples.english: A small sum of money went missing from the office last night.
examples.chinese: 昨晚办公室有一笔金额不大的钱不见了。

examples.english: The man had reported his girlfriend missing three days after she disappeared.
examples.chinese: 女朋友失踪三天以后，那人报了警。

examples.english: I've looked everywhere for the car keys. I think they must be lost.
examples.chinese: 车钥匙我到处都找过了，我想肯定是丢了。

examples.english: Divers are searching for the plane's lost flight recorder.
examples.chinese: 潜水员正在寻找飞机上丢失的飞行记录器。

examples.english: You haven't gotten my letter yet? It must have gotten lost in the mail.
examples.chinese: 你还没收到我的信？肯定是邮寄过程中遗失了。

examples.english: I thought I had a copy of the notes, but they seem to have disappeared somewhere.
examples.chinese: 我记得我有一份这样的笔记，但不知道到哪里去了。

examples.english: The parents of an art student who disappeared in the middle of his exams have made an emotional plea for him to come home.
examples.chinese: 一名艺术系学生考试中途出走，他的父母伤心地恳求他回家。

examples.english: The letter had mysteriously disappeared from the file overnight.
examples.chinese: 那封信一夜之间从档案里神秘消失了。

examples.english: It's now three months since his disappearance.
examples.chinese: 他已经失踪三个月了。

examples.english: Our tour guide was nowhere to be seen, so we set off to explore the city alone.
examples.chinese: 我们的导游不见了，所以我们就自己在城里逛逛。

examples.english: She'd looked everywhere for her glasses, but they were nowhere to be found.
examples.chinese: 她到处找眼镜，哪里也找不到。

centers.english: to lose a game, argument, election, war etc
centers.chinese: 输掉比赛、争论、竞选、战争等

examples.english: I always lose when I play tennis with my sister.
examples.chinese: 我和姐姐打网球总是我输。

examples.english: Everyone expected the Democrats to lose the election.
examples.chinese: 大家都预料民主党会败选。

examples.english: The Chicago Bears lost their eighth game in a row they lost eight games, one after the other.
examples.chinese: 芝加哥熊队连续第八场输了比赛。

examples.english: England lost to Brazil in the final.
examples.chinese: 决赛中英格兰队输给了巴西队。

examples.english: He lost his title unexpectedly to a man who is virtually unknown outside boxing circles.
examples.chinese: 他出人意料地把他的冠军称号输给了拳坛以外几乎无人认识的一个人。

examples.english: The Springboks lost by only three points to the All Blacks.
examples.chinese: “跳羚”队仅以三分之差输给“全黑”队。

examples.english: The match was lost 3 to 1.
examples.chinese: 比赛以1比3落败。

examples.english: Many people think that the Democrats’ tax policies lost them the election.
examples.chinese: 许多人认为民主党的税收政策导致了他们的落选。

examples.english: In 37 fights, Lewis has only been beaten once.
examples.chinese: 37场拳击赛中，刘易斯只被打败过一次。

examples.english: Jake sure doesn't like being beaten.
examples.chinese: 杰克当然不希望输掉。

examples.english: The Barbarians were beaten in the quarter-finals by the Korean side.
examples.chinese: 野蛮人队在四分之一决赛中被韩国队击败。

examples.english: We always seems to get beaten when we play in Europe.
examples.chinese: 我们在欧洲比赛好像总是输。

examples.english: Government forces took control of the town after the rebel forces were defeated.
examples.chinese: 叛军被击溃以后，政府军占领了小镇。

examples.english: Last night, the Raiders were defeated by a superior team.
examples.chinese: 昨晚“突袭者”队被一支技高一筹的球队打败。

examples.english: He ran for Congress last year, but was decisively defeated.
examples.chinese: 去年他参加了国会竞选，却遭到惨败。

examples.english: The Democratic Party was narrowly defeated in the general election, and will form a coalition with the Congress party.
examples.chinese: 民主党在大选中以很小的差距落败，将与国会党组成联盟。

examples.english: It was the Christian Democratic Party's fourth successive electoral defeat.
examples.chinese: 这是基督教民主党连续第四次竞选失败。

examples.english: the defeat of Napoleon at the battle of Waterloo
examples.chinese: 拿破仑在滑铁卢战役中的溃败

examples.english: Scotland's defeat of Spain
examples.chinese: 苏格兰队挫败西班牙队

examples.english: Mr Taylor blamed bad publicity for his defeat by Mr Jones.
examples.chinese: 泰勒先生把自己被琼斯先生击败归因于宣传不力。

examples.english: The captain offered no excuses for his team's humiliating defeat.
examples.chinese: 队长对于这场丢脸的败仗没有作出任何辩解。

examples.english: The New York Times described the withdrawal of troops as a resounding defeat for the government.
examples.chinese: 《纽约时报》称这次撤军是政府的惨败。

examples.english: She retired from the sport after suffering a series of humiliating defeats.
examples.chinese: 她遭受一连串丢人的失败之后退出了体坛。

examples.english: She's a good player but she'll meet her match when she plays Sara.
examples.chinese: 她是个非常优秀的选手，但与萨拉较量算是遇上了对手。

examples.english: I slowly started to realize I had met my match in Nigel.
examples.chinese: 我渐渐意识到，遇到奈杰尔，我是棋逢对手了。

examples.english: ‘I hear you took a bit of a beating last night.’ ‘Yeah, we lost 12 -2.’
examples.chinese: “听说你们昨天晚上输得很惨。”“是啊，我们2比12输掉了。”

centers.english: to stop trying to win

examples.english: The four climbers were eventually forced to admit defeat when weather conditions made it impossible to continue.
examples.chinese: 天气条件使那四名登山运动员无法继续前行，最后被迫认输。

examples.english: She simply refuses to accept defeat, no matter how bad things seem.
examples.chinese: 不管情况看起来有多糟，她就是不肯认输。

examples.english: Marie's stubborn, and she doesn't give in easily.
examples.chinese: 玛丽很固执，不会轻易认输。

examples.english: I figured if we gave in to them this time, they'd be back for another fight.
examples.chinese: 我估计如果我们这一次向他们屈服，他们还会回来找我们再战。

examples.english: In the end, I had to give in to dad -- he's usually right anyway.
examples.chinese: 最后，我只得屈从于爸爸一—无论如何，他通常是对的。

examples.english: Finally, on April 16th, the enemy surrendered.
examples.chinese: 敌军最终于4月16日投降。

examples.english: 19 rebels hiding in the Czech embassy surrendered to the authorities.
examples.chinese: 躲在捷克大使馆里的19名叛乱分子向当局投降。

examples.english: The Milanese were starving, and forced into surrender.
examples.chinese: 米兰市民在挨饿，他们被迫投降。

examples.english: Colonel Casado was anxious to negotiate a surrender.
examples.chinese: 卡萨多上校急于谈判请降。

examples.english: General Haig would accept nothing less than unconditional surrender.
examples.chinese: 黑格将军只愿接受无条件投降。

examples.english: Eventually, the chairman was forced to concede and announce his resignation.
examples.chinese: 最后，主席被迫认输，宣布辞职。

examples.english: ‘Sam, you have to apologise to her’ -- ‘I know,’ Sam conceded grudgingly.
examples.chinese: “萨姆，你得向她道歉。”“知道了。”萨姆很不情愿地屈服了。

examples.english: Matthew kept on arguing his point, unwilling to concede defeat.
examples.chinese: 马修为自己的观点辩个不休，不肯认输。

examples.english: Environmentalists concede that it will not be easy to persuade car drivers to use their vehicles less often.
examples.chinese: 环保主义者承认，要说服驾车的人少用汽车并非易事。

examples.english: We both had a point, but neither of us would concede to the other.
examples.chinese: 我们俩各持己见，互不相让。

centers.english: when you are likely to lose a game etc

examples.english: ‘Is Joachim winning?’ ‘No, he's losing.’
examples.chinese: “乔基姆会赢吗？”“不，他要输了。”

examples.english: It can be difficult not to show your frustration when you're losing the match.
examples.chinese: 比赛要输的时候很难隐藏失望的心情。

examples.english: ‘What's the score?’ ‘We're losing 3 -- nil.’
examples.chinese: “比分是多少？”“我们0比3落后。”

expressions.text: be behind by two goals/by10 points etc

examples.english: The opinion polls show that the Republicans are behind by 3%.
examples.chinese: 民意测验显示，共和党落后3%。

examples.english: ‘How are we doing?’ ‘We're 10 points behind, I'm afraid.’
examples.chinese: “我们成绩怎么样？”“恐怕我们落后10分。”

expressions.text: be a long way behind/be way behind

examples.english: Although we only had a short break, we were a long way behind the front runners.
examples.chinese: 虽然我们只短暂地休息了一会儿，但是我们比领先的赛跑运动员落后了一大截。

examples.english: The Eagles are way behind. We'll never win at this rate.
examples.chinese: 鹰队远远落后，照这样下去我们赢不了。

examples.english: Penn State trail West Virginia by only 1 point.
examples.chinese: 宾州队仅以1分之差落后于西弗吉尼亚队。

examples.english: Sweden was trailing by 2 games to 1.
examples.chinese: 瑞典队以二负一胜落后。

examples.english: With his team trailing 72--78, the manager was forced to bring back his star player.
examples.chinese: 球队以72比78落后时，领队只得重新派他的明星球员上场。

examples.english: These two top drivers have left the others trailing behind.
examples.chinese: 这两名领先的车手已经把其他人甩在了后面。

examples.english: Labour trailed third, behind the Conservative and Liberal parties.
examples.chinese: 工党落后于保守党和自由党，屈居第三。

examples.english: The defending champion was beaten by an outsider in the first round.
examples.chinese: 卫冕冠军在第一回合被一个无名小卒打败了。

examples.english: Smith, a little-known outsider with limited political experience, came from behind to score a surprise victory.
examples.chinese: 史密斯—一匹鲜为人知的政治黑马，从政经验有限，却后来居上意外获胜。

examples.english: He started as a no-hoper -- a rank outsider for the title.
examples.chinese: 他开始的时候毫无希望，根本无缘取得冠军。

centers.english: someone who has lost

examples.english: The losers walked slowly off the field.
examples.chinese: 输掉比赛的一方慢慢地离开场地。

examples.english: I'd like to congratulate all our entrants, the winners and the losers.
examples.chinese: 我要祝贺我们所有的参赛者，不管是赢的还是输的。

examples.english: You're just a bad loser Phil, that's your problem.
examples.chinese: 你是个输不起的人，菲尔，这是你的问题所在。

examples.english: Nobody wants to be on the losing side.
examples.chinese: 没有人想在输球的球队里。

examples.english: The quiz show gives losing contestants the chance to see what they would have won.
examples.chinese: 这个问答比赛节目给输掉比赛的参赛者一个机会，让他们看看自己本来可以拿到什么奖品。

examples.english: 100 lucky runners-up will receive a boxed set of CDs.
examples.chinese: 除第一名之外的100名幸运的得奖者将获得一套盒装CD唱片。

examples.english: Gordon was also presented with a cheque as runner-up of the closely fought contest.
examples.chinese: 在这场旗鼓相当的比赛中，戈登作为第二名也获得了一张支票。

examples.english: Let's have a quick word with the two defeated finalists.
examples.chinese: 我们和两位在决赛中失利的选手说几句话吧。

examples.english: The rebels hope to weaken the regime further by attacking its defeated and demoralised forces.
examples.chinese: 叛军向士气已丧的溃军发动了袭击，希望借此再度削弱该政权的力量。

centers.english: a group of people together in one place

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: A small group had gathered outside the stage door.
examples.chinese: 剧场后门聚集了一小群人。

examples.english: Outside the school, little groups of friends were talking to each other.
examples.chinese: 在学校外面，朋友之间三三两两地在互相交谈。

examples.english: an old photograph of a group of soldiers sitting on the ground
examples.chinese: 一张一群士兵坐在地上的旧照片

examples.english: Men stood in groups on street corners.
examples.chinese: 一些男人三五成群地站在街角上。

examples.english: The teacher told us to get into groups of three.
examples.chinese: 老师叫我们分成三人一组。

examples.english: A huge crowd gathered to hear Mandela speak.
examples.chinese: 一大群人聚集起来听曼德拉发表演说。

examples.english: a football crowd
examples.chinese: 足球观众

examples.english: A crowd of reporters were waiting for her at the airport.
examples.chinese: 一群记者在机场等候着她。

examples.english: I walked down Regent Street with its crowds of tourists and Christmas shoppers.
examples.chinese: 我随着游客和圣诞节购物者的人流走在摄政街上。

examples.english: A cluster of people, all anxious to shake his hand, formed around the speaker.
examples.chinese: 一群人围在演讲者的身边，都急着想跟他握手。

examples.english: Some relatives were standing in a cluster around her hospital bed.
examples.chinese: 医院里，几个家属紧挨着围在她的病床边。

examples.english: The knot of men at the bar had started talking about the elections.
examples.chinese: 酒吧里，几个男人开始谈论起竞选来。

examples.english: Outside the hotel, a little knot of bystanders had gathered to see what was happening.
examples.chinese: 旅馆外面聚集了一小群人围观所发生的事。

centers.english: a group of people who do things together

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: The tickets are expensive, but there is a discount for school groups.
examples.chinese: 票价很贵，但是学校团体有折扣。

examples.english: A group of us went out for a drink to celebrate Sonia's birthday.
examples.chinese: 我们一群人出去喝一杯，庆祝索尼娅的生日。

examples.english: Robberies were common on the lonely roads, so people usually travelled in groups.
examples.chinese: 偏僻的道路上抢劫事件很多，所以人们一般都结伴出行。

examples.english: We were warned not to walk in the mountains except in groups of three or more.
examples.chinese: 有人提醒我们，没有三人或以上不要在山里行走。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: A party of Japanese businessmen will be visiting the factory next week.
examples.chinese: 一队日本商人将于下周来工厂访问。

examples.english: John was taking a party of tourists around the museum.
examples.chinese: 约翰正在带领一群游客参观博物馆。

examples.english: The climbers did not return, and a search party was sent out to look for them.
examples.chinese: 登山队员没有回来，于是派出搜索队去寻找他们。

examples.english: The people on my French course are a really friendly bunch.
examples.chinese: 我法语课上的那帮人非常友好。

examples.english: There was the usual crowd of students standing at the bar.
examples.chinese: 站在酒吧里的又是往常的那一群学生。

examples.english: Willy's band is playing tonight, and I invited a bunch of people to come along
examples.chinese: 威利的乐队今晚要演出，我请了一群人来捧场。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: Inner-city kids often join gangs for protection, and for the chance to make money by selling drugs.
examples.chinese: 旧城区的孩子经常加入团伙寻求保护，以及寻找出售毒品挣钱的机会。

examples.english: There are always gangs of kids hanging around the shopping mall.
examples.chinese: 购物中心里总是有一帮帮年轻人在游荡。

examples.english: It is not just gang members who get into trouble - it's middle-class and upper-class kids as well.
examples.chinese: 惹事的不仅仅是帮派成员—还有中产阶级和上层阶级的年轻人。

examples.english: Fighting broke out between two rival gangs.
examples.chinese: 两个敌对的帮派之间发生了打斗。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: Not surprisingly, there was a large student contingent at the demonstration.
examples.chinese: 毫不奇怪，游行队伍里有一个很大的学生代表团。

examples.english: There was a large American contingent, including the Olympic bronze medallist, Thomas Jefferson.
examples.chinese: 美国来了个很大的代表团，其中有奥运会铜牌得主托马斯杰斐逊。

examples.english: A small contingent of English fans had made the trip to Sydney to support their team.
examples.chinese: 一个小小的英格兰球迷代表团前往悉尼支持自己的球队。

centers.english: a group of people who are similar or have similar ideas
centers.chinese: 相似或具有相似观点的一群人

examples.english: Their policy was to keep demonstrators from different political groups apart.
examples.chinese: 他们的政策是把游行示威的人和不同的政治团体区分开来。

examples.english: The factory was burned down by a group of animal-rights activists.
examples.chinese: 工厂是被一个积极支持动物权利的团体烧掉的。

examples.english: I don't like her new friends - they're a snobbish lot.
examples.chinese: 我不喜欢她的新朋友，他们是一群势利鬼。

examples.english: We've got another lot of visitors coming this weekend.
examples.chinese: 这个周末，我们又有另一批客人来。

examples.english: Don't take any notice of that lot, they're just ignorant.
examples.chinese: 不要理那帮家伙，他们很无知。

examples.english: There was an interesting collection of people at the wedding.
examples.chinese: 婚礼上有一群有趣的人。

examples.english: Jack brought an odd collection of characters home from the racetrack.
examples.chinese: 杰克从赛车场上把一群古怪的人带回家。

examples.english: We shared the train compartment with an odd assortment of fellow travellers.
examples.chinese: 我们和一群各色各样古里古怪的同路人同坐在一个火车包厢里。

examples.english: The chief had been warned that there were criminal elements within the Security Police.
examples.chinese: 有人警告警长说保安警察中有犯罪分子。

examples.english: They seem to be trying to get rid of all left-wing elements in the party.
examples.chinese: 他们似乎想清除党内所有的左翼分子。

examples.english: The committee agreed on the need to get rid of the hooligan element amongst football supporters.
examples.chinese: 委员会同意有必要清除足球迷中的流氓分子。

examples.english: She was active in a number of political movements, including the campaign to end slavery.
examples.chinese: 她积极投入一些政治运动，包括废奴运动。

examples.english: One of the leaders of the pro-democracy movement has been arrested.
examples.chinese: 支持民主运动的其中一位领导人被捕了。

examples.english: The aim of the civil rights movement was to eliminate racial discrimination in all its forms.
examples.chinese: 民权运动的目的是消灭各种形式的种族歧视。

examples.english: There is no denying the influence of the Impressionist school in his painting.
examples.chinese: 无可否认，他的画受到印象派的影响。

examples.english: He seems to be very much part of the Marxist school in his approach to politics.
examples.chinese: 他的政治方向似乎在很大程度上属于马克思主义流派。

examples.english: One school of thought argues that introducing stiffer penalties would bring the crime rate down.
examples.chinese: 有一派人认为推行更严厉的刑罚会降低犯罪率。

examples.english: By 1920 she had written two novels, and had succeeded in winning recognition in literary circles.
examples.chinese: 到1920年的时候她已经写了两部小说，并得到了文学界的表扬。

examples.english: It was the 1960s, and the military had become unpopular among academic and intellectual circles.
examples.chinese: 那是在20世纪60年代，军队已经在学术界和知识界失去了支持。

examples.english: The latest batch of new recruits had just arrived at the camp.
examples.chinese: 最近的一批新兵刚到达营地。

centers.english: a group of people who work together

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: There will be a meeting for all members of the team next Wednesday.
examples.chinese: 下星期三小组全体成员将召开一次会议。

examples.english: Dr Gaultier and his medical team worked in the refugee camps for over a year.
examples.chinese: 戈尔捷医生和他的医疗小组在难民营里工作了一年多。

examples.english: The coins were discovered by a team of archaeologists.
examples.chinese: 这些钱币是一个考古小组发现的。

examples.english: The Prime Minister works closely with a team of unelected advisers.
examples.chinese: 首相和一个非选举产生的顾问小组密切配合。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: The captain and crew would like to welcome you on board Flight 381 to Geneva.
examples.chinese: 机长和机组人员欢迎各位乘坐飞往日内瓦的381号航班。

examples.english: Everyone aboard the Montreal Queen, including the crew, died.
examples.chinese: 蒙特利尔皇后号的所有人，包括船员，全部遇难。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: Bill Dean has been elected chairman of the committee.
examples.chinese: 比尔·迪安已当选为委员会主席。

examples.english: The finance committee has decided to raise membership fees for next season.
examples.chinese: 财务委员会决定提高下一季的会费。

examples.english: She's been on the Church committee for 20 years.
examples.chinese: 她任教会委员会委员已经有20年了。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: The licensing board has refused us permission to sell alcohol on the premises.
examples.chinese: 执照委员会不准许我们在场内卖酒。

examples.english: In October, the school board recommended that uniforms become compulsory.
examples.chinese: 10月，学校委员会建议学生必须穿校服。

examples.english: If you don't agree with the result etc ,you can appeal to the board of examiners.
examples.chinese: 如果你对成绩有异议，可以向考试委员会上诉。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: I've been invited to join the panel on a radio arts programme.
examples.chinese: 我被邀请参加一个广播艺术节目的讨论小组。

examples.english: All applicants are questioned by a panel of experienced interviewers.
examples.chinese: 所有申请人都要接受一组资深考官的提问。

examples.english: He was on a panel of judges for a famous literary prize.
examples.chinese: 他是一个著名的文学奖评判小组的成员。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: A Police Force working party concluded that many people found the police intimidating.
examples.chinese: 一个警察部门的工作组得出结论—很多人觉得警察令人望而生畏。

examples.english: A working party was set up to investigate and act on the report's findings.
examples.chinese: 成立了一个工作组根据报告中的发现进行调查并采取行动。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: Hospital officials plan to use the donations to set up a new cardiac unit.
examples.chinese: 医院行政人员计划用捐款设立一个新的心脏科。

examples.english: One of the policemen from the drug unit will be visiting local schools.
examples.chinese: 缉毒科的一名警员将访问当地学校。

centers.english: a group of people who are the same age, have the same income etc
centers.chinese: 年龄、收入等相同的一群人

examples.english: The good thing about the class is that all the students belong to the same age group.
examples.chinese: 这个班的好处在于所有的学生都属于同一年龄组。

examples.english: Families in the lowest income group could not afford to educate their children.
examples.chinese: 最低收入家庭负担不起孩子的教育。

examples.english: The hospital is desperate for donors from the rhesus negative blood group.
examples.chinese: 那家医院急于从Rh阴性血型的人中找到捐血者。

examples.english: Several companies have produced pension plans specifically for people in the higher income tax bracket.
examples.chinese: 有几家公司为纳税等级较高的人制定了特别的养老金计划。

examples.english: These changes will not affect people in the lowest tax band.
examples.chinese: 这些变化不会影响最低税级的人。!

examples.english: As you move into the higher income bands, the charges start to increase.
examples.chinese: 随着收入提高，各种应付款项也开始增加了。

centers.english: a small group of people who are unwilling to let other people join them
centers.chinese: 不愿意让别人加入的一小群人

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: Most of the kids were friendly, apart from a clique of girls who came from rich families.
examples.chinese: 除了来自有钱人家的那一小撮女孩子之外，大多数孩子都很友好。

examples.english: a clique of literary friends who thought they were so superior
examples.chinese: 优越感很强的一个文人朋友圈子

examples.english: The tennis club has good facilities, but it's terribly cliquey.
examples.chinese: 这个网球俱乐部设施很好，但是太排外了。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: Only a small elite can afford to send their children to this school.
examples.chinese: 只有一小群上层人士负担得起送孩子来这所学校上学。

examples.english: All the glamorous Washington elite were at the dinner that evening.
examples.chinese: 华盛顿所有富有魅力的精英人士都出席了那天晚上的宴会。

examples.english: Anyone who studied at the college joined an elite band of well-connected lawyers, doctors and businessmen.
examples.chinese: 那所大学所有的学生都加入一个精英圈子，这个圈子里都是一些社会关系很广泛的律师、医生和商业界人士。

examples.english: That's the nightclub where all the in-crowd go.
examples.chinese: 所有这个圈子里的人都去那家夜总会。

examples.english: He wanted to be accepted, to be part of the in-crowd.
examples.chinese: 他希望被接受，希望成为这个小圈子里的一分子。

examples.english: Within the political in-crowd are some extremely influential figures.
examples.chinese: 那个政治小集团有一些极有影响力的人物。

centers.english: a small group within a larger political or religious organization
centers.chinese: 较大的政治或宗教组织中的一个小团体

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: The pro-war faction within the party condemned any attempt at negotiation.
examples.chinese: 党内的主战派指责任何谈判行动。

examples.english: The whole of the country has been taken over and destroyed by warring factions.
examples.chinese: 全国各地都被占领，派系混战摧毁了整个国家。

examples.english: The terrorist fringe condemned the decision and threatened to use force.
examples.chinese: 恐怖主义的极端分子谴责了这项决定，并威胁说要使用武力。

examples.english: Crusading journalist William Lloyd Garrison represented the radical fringe.
examples.chinese: 斗志昂扬的新闻工作者威廉·劳埃德·加里森代表激进的这一派。

examples.english: a lunatic fringe of cranks and reactionaries, who probably still believe that the earth is flat
examples.chinese: 一群可能还相信地球是扁平的极端怪人和反动分子

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: They broke away and formed a splinter group that believed in revolution, not gradual change.
examples.chinese: 他们脱离出去另立了一个相信革命、不相信逐渐变革的派别。

examples.english: The splinter group rapidly gained support from discontented members.
examples.chinese: 分裂出去的这个派别很快得到成员中不满者的支持。

centers.english: a group of people who have been chosen to give information, answer questions etc
centers.chinese: 被挑选出来提供信息、回答问题的一群人

examples.english: 56% of the women in the sample said that they supported the government's policies.
examples.chinese: 被抽选的女性中有56%说她们支持政府的政策。

examples.english: These responses were drawn from a random sample of the electorate.
examples.chinese: 这些回答是从选民中随机抽取的。

examples.english: She based her analysis on data from a representative sample of women and men aged 18-25.
examples.chinese: 她的分析用的是从年龄在18至25岁之间具有代表性的男女抽样中获得的资料。

examples.english: We use focus groups, surveys, and questionnaires to try to gauge our customers’ needs.
examples.chinese: 我们利用焦点小组、调查和问卷的形式来判断客户的需要。

examples.english: Focus groups revealed that teenagers who had higher self-confidence were more likely to use contraception.
examples.chinese: 焦点小组调查发现，自信心较强的青少年更有可能采取避孕措施。

centers.english: when people come together to form a group

expressions.text: form a group/get into a group

examples.english: We formed groups, and discussed the text together.
examples.chinese: 我们分组一起讨论课文。

examples.english: Several people formed a group round the speaker.
examples.chinese: 几个人围拢在演讲者周围。

expressions.text: form/get into groups of three/four etc

examples.english: Get into groups of three for this exercise.
examples.chinese: 做这个练习要分成三人一组。

examples.english: Students grouped around the notice board to read their exam results.
examples.chinese: 学生们围在通告栏前看自己的考试成绩。

examples.english: Julia sat down at the piano, and the others grouped together to sing.
examples.chinese: 朱莉娅在钢琴旁边坐下，其他人聚在一起唱歌。

examples.english: I looked down onto the square, where a large crowd had assembled.
examples.chinese: 我朝广场望去，那里已聚集了一大群人。

examples.english: A large crowd had assembled opposite the American embassy.
examples.chinese: 美国大使馆对面聚集了一大群人。

examples.english: A group of angry parents had assembled outside the head teacher's office.
examples.chinese: 一群愤怒的家长聚集在校长办公室外面。

examples.english: A group of beggars were huddling in the shop entrance to keep dry.
examples.chinese: 一群乞丐挤在商店门口避雨。

examples.english: The room didn't have any heating, and we had to huddle together for warmth.
examples.chinese: 房间里没有暖气，我们只得挤在一起取暖。

examples.english: Children had clustered outside the shop window to look at the toys on display.
examples.chinese: 小孩子凑在橱窗外面看陈列着的玩具。

examples.english: The nurses were clustered together in the corridor, giggling about something.
examples.chinese: 护士们聚集在走廊上，为着什么事情咯咯发笑。

centers.english: a number of things thought of as a group
centers.chinese: 被视为一组的一些东西

examples.english: The house was hidden behind a tall group of trees.
examples.chinese: 房子隐藏在一片高大的树林后面。

examples.english: News International is a group of companies that produce newspapers and TV programmes.
examples.chinese: 新闻国际是一个有多家报纸和电视节目制作公司的集团。

examples.english: A group of new houses is to be built on the old playing-field.
examples.chinese: 旧操场上将建造一组新房子。

examples.english: Today you will learn a new group of verbs.
examples.chinese: 今天你们将学习一组新的动词。

expressions.gram: countable noun with singular or plural verb in British English

examples.english: a chess set
examples.chinese: 一套国际象棋

examples.english: a cutlery set
examples.chinese: 一套餐具

examples.english: Amy bought him a set of tools for metal and woodworking.
examples.chinese: 埃米给他买了一套做金工和木工用的工具。

examples.english: I gave a spare set of house keys to my neighbours.
examples.chinese: 我把家里的一套备用钥匙给了邻居。

examples.english: The first set of questions wasn't too bad, but they got really difficult after that.
examples.chinese: 第一组问题不是太糟，但是后面的就非常难了。

examples.english: We started the meeting by agreeing on a set of objectives.
examples.chinese: 我们一开始开会就确定了一组目标。

examples.english: a collection of ancient Greek coins
examples.chinese: 一批古希腊硬币收藏品

examples.english: The museum has a superb collection of Mexican pottery.
examples.chinese: 博物馆里藏有一批精美的墨西哥陶器。

examples.english: Have you seen her CD collection - it's enormous!
examples.chinese: 你见过她收集的CD唱片吗—数量非常多！

examples.english: She handed me a bag of old clothes. ‘Could you take this lot to the charity shop for me?’
examples.chinese: 她递给我一包旧衣服，“请你帮我把这包东西送到慈善商店去好吗？”

examples.english: Right lads, let's pick up this lot and go home.
examples.chinese: 好吧，小伙子们，让我们把这些东西收起来回家吧。

examples.english: Bring that lot over here, will you?
examples.chinese: 把那些玩意儿拿过来，好吗？

examples.english: On the floor was an assortment of boxes and packages.
examples.chinese: 地板上是一堆各种各样的盒子和包裹。

examples.english: The soldier received a parcel containing an assortment of shirts, biscuits, and canned food.
examples.chinese: 这个士兵收到了一个包裹，里面有衬衫、饼干和罐头食品等各种各样的东西。

examples.english: From the airplane we could already see little clusters of houses.
examples.chinese: 从飞机上我们已经能看到一簇簇的房子。

examples.english: It's an attractive shrub with dark shiny leaves, that has clusters of white flowers in early June.
examples.chinese: 那是一丛美丽的灌木，叶子深绿得发亮，在6月初已经开满了一簇簇白色的花儿。

examples.english: The adult female lays large clusters of eggs.
examples.chinese: 成年雌体产下大堆大堆的卵。

examples.english: Most galaxies are found in clusters rather than in isolation.
examples.chinese: 大多数星系都成星团存在，而不是孤立的。

examples.english: Sort the files into batches and give one batch to each secretary.
examples.chinese: 把文件一份份地整理好，然后给每个秘书一份。

examples.english: The baker took a batch of freshly baked rolls out of the oven.
examples.chinese: 面包师从烤炉里拿出刚烤好的一炉面包卷。

examples.english: Mr Green, I have a batch of letters here for you to sign.
examples.chinese: 格林先生，我这里有一批信要你签名。

centers.english: a group of things that are tied together or fastened together
centers.chinese: 被捆在一起或系在一起的一组东西

examples.english: She keeps all his old letters, tied up in bundles.
examples.chinese: 她把他所有的旧信件一捆捆地扎好并保存下来。

examples.english: a bundle of twigs
examples.chinese: 一束嫩枝

examples.english: He put his hand in his pocket, and pulled out a large bundle of £50 notes.
examples.chinese: 他把手伸进衣袋，掏出一大叠50英镑的钞票。

examples.english: He handed her a huge bunch of roses.
examples.chinese: 他递给她一大束玫瑰。

examples.english: Has anyone seen a bunch of keys?
examples.chinese: 有人看见一串钥匙吗？

examples.english: I bought a kilo of apples and a bunch of grapes.
examples.chinese: 我买了一公斤苹果和一串葡萄。

examples.english: I saw him trying to press a wad of cash into the woman's hand. She wouldn't take it.
examples.chinese: 我看见他想把一沓钱塞进那女人的手里，她不肯拿。

examples.english: She opened her handbag, and pulled out a wad of banknotes.
examples.chinese: 她打开手提包，取出一叠钞票。

examples.english: He had a sheaf of papers under his arm.
examples.chinese: 他腋下夹着一叠报纸。

examples.english: I saw her put a sheaf of notes into her briefcase.
examples.chinese: 我看见她把一叠钞票放进公文包里。

centers.english: a group of things on top of each other
centers.chinese: 一层层叠放的一组东西

examples.english: Put those letters on the other pile.
examples.chinese: 把那些信放在另外一叠上。

examples.english: Can you separate those out into two piles - A to L and M to Z, please?
examples.chinese: 你把那些分成A到L和M到Z两叠好吗?

examples.english: Greg carried the pile of ironed shirts upstairs.
examples.chinese: 格雷格把一叠熨好的衬衫捧到楼上去。

examples.english: Her office is a terrible mess - there are piles of papers all over the floor.
examples.chinese: 她的办公室乱极了——地上到处都是一堆堆的文件。

examples.english: a pile of dirty dishes
examples.chinese: 一堆脏碟子

examples.english: The books were arranged in neat piles on her desk.
examples.chinese: 她的桌上放着一叠叠整齐的书。

examples.english: There was a huge heap of blankets and pillows on the bed.
examples.chinese: 床上有一大堆毯子和枕头。

examples.english: The kids left all their wet towels in a heap on the bathroom floor.
examples.chinese: 孩子们把湿毛巾都堆在卫生间的地上。

examples.english: The whole stack fell over, and half the plates got broken.
examples.chinese: 整叠盘子都倒在地上，有一半被打碎了。

examples.english: Next to the bottles was a tall stack of plastic cups.
examples.chinese: 瓶子的旁边是一堆叠得高高的塑料杯。

examples.english: a stack of sales brochures
examples.chinese: 一叠商品小册子

examples.english: The Grand Hotel was now just a mound of rubble.
examples.chinese: 格兰德大酒店现在成了一大堆瓦砾。

examples.english: A mound of leaves is the perfect place for a hedgehog to hibernate.
examples.chinese: 一大堆树叶是刺猬冬眠最理想的地方。

centers.english: to put things or people into groups

examples.english: It takes a couple of hours to sort the mail in the morning.
examples.chinese: 上午整理邮件要花好几个小时。

examples.english: We sorted all the clothes into two piles - those to be kept, and those to be given away.
examples.chinese: 我们把所有的衣服分成两堆—一堆留着，一堆要送掉。

examples.english: The rubbish has to be sorted into things that can be recycled and things that can't
examples.chinese: 垃圾必须分成可以回收的和不可以回收的两种。

examples.english: The eggs are sorted according to size.
examples.chinese: 这些鸡蛋根据大小分类。

examples.english: Communication involves a variety of behaviours which are difficult to categorise.
examples.chinese: 沟通涉及多种行为，很难归类。

examples.english: The hotels are categorized according to the standard of the rooms and services they offer.
examples.chinese: 宾馆根据它们所提供的客房和服务的标准分类。

examples.english: Words can be categorised into verbs, nouns, adjectives etc.
examples.chinese: 单词可以分为动词、名词、形容词等。

examples.english: Animals are categorised into three types - carnivores, herbivores and omnivores.
examples.chinese: 动物分成三类—食肉动物、食草动物和杂食动物。

examples.english: The store categorizes records from Asia and Africa as ‘World Music’.
examples.chinese: 这家商店把来自亚洲和非洲的唱片归类为“世界音乐”。

examples.english: Scientists have discovered a new type of butterfly which has not yet been classified.
examples.chinese: 科学家发现了一种新的蝴蝶，目前尚未归类。

examples.english: Babies walking later than 18 months were classified as slow walkers.
examples.chinese: 晚于18个月走路的婴儿被归为走得晚的人。

examples.english: There is no common European standard for the classification of hotels.
examples.chinese: 欧洲没有通用的标准给宾馆分级。

examples.english: The vehicles are grouped according to engine size.
examples.chinese: 这些车辆根据引擎的大小分类。

examples.english: Non-fiction books are grouped together under different subjects.
examples.chinese: 非小说类书籍按照不同科目归类。

examples.english: Most European languages can be grouped into two main families.
examples.chinese: 大多数欧洲语言都可以归入两个主要语系。

examples.english: The respondents were grouped into three categories - non-smokers, smokers, and ex-smokers.
examples.chinese: 受访者分为三类一不抽烟的、抽烟的和曾经抽过烟的。

examples.english: This prison houses the most dangerous criminals in Britain, those classed as "category A'.
examples.chinese: 这所监狱关押着英国最危险的罪犯，即被列为“A级”的罪犯。

examples.english: Heroin and cocaine are classed as hard drugs.
examples.chinese: 海洛因和可卡因被列为烈性毒品。

examples.english: All the fruit is taken to the warehouse where it is graded and packed.
examples.chinese: 所有水果都会运到仓库里分级和包装。

examples.english: One supermarket now grades its wines on a scale of 1 to 9, from driest to sweetest.
examples.chinese: 现在有一家超级市场将葡萄酒从最不甜到最甜分成1到9九个等级。

centers.english: a party

examples.english: We're having a party at my house. Do you want to come?
examples.chinese: 我们家里要举行聚会，你愿意来吗?

examples.english: Did you go to Stella's party?
examples.chinese: 你参加了斯特拉的聚会吗？

examples.english: How many people have they invited to the party?
examples.chinese: 他们邀请了多少人来参加聚会？

examples.english: We gave her a surprise party at a local bar.
examples.chinese: 我们在当地的酒吧为她举办一场惊喜聚会。

examples.english: Over a hundred children came to the annual Christmas party.
examples.chinese: 一百多个孩子前来参加一年一度的圣诞晚会。

examples.english: I gave my first dinner party last weekend.
examples.chinese: 上周末我第一次举行了晚宴。

examples.english: Office parties are fun if you're young, free, and single.
examples.chinese: 要是你年轻、有空又是单身的话，办公室聚会挺有趣的。

examples.english: You're invited to a fancy dress party.
examples.chinese: 敬请参加化装晚会。

examples.english: I went to a cocktail party in the lobby of the Ritz once.
examples.chinese: 我在里兹酒店的大堂参加过一次鸡尾酒会。

examples.english: Shana's picture won first prize, so we had a little get-together to celebrate.
examples.chinese: 莎娜的画拿了一等奖，所以我们举办了一次小小的聚会以示庆贺。

examples.english: a big family get-together
examples.chinese: 家庭大聚会

examples.english: A friend of mine's having a bit of a do in town tomorrow night.
examples.chinese: 我的一个朋友明晚在镇上要举行晚会。

examples.english: Are you going to Darren's leaving do?
examples.chinese: 你参加巴伦的欢送会吗？

examples.english: We're giving a shower for Beth next week.
examples.chinese: 下周我们为贝丝举行送礼会。

examples.english: I want to thank both of you again for your beautiful shower gifts.
examples.chinese: 我想再次感谢你们俩在送礼会上给我那么漂亮的礼物。

examples.english: What did you give Chris for her wedding shower?
examples.chinese: 克丽丝的结婚送礼会上你送了什么?

examples.english: We didn't play any of the usual games at the baby shower.
examples.chinese: 分娩送礼会上我们没有玩平常的游戏。

examples.english: The band are flying out to Ibiza tonight for a huge four-day celebrity bash.
examples.chinese: 该乐队准备今晚飞往伊维萨岛参加为期四天的大型庆祝狂欢活动。

centers.english: a formal or official party

examples.english: The Lavender Room can be booked for functions or parties.
examples.chinese: 举办重大宴会或聚会，可以预订“紫厅”。

examples.english: His specialist service is in constant demand for big corporate functions.
examples.chinese: 大型公司宴会经常需要他的专业服务。

examples.english: Part of her duties is attending official state functions.
examples.chinese: 她的部分职责是出席官方的国宴。

examples.english: On the second night, the captain always holds a formal reception for the crew and passengers.
examples.chinese: 船长每次都在第二天晚上为船员和乘客举行正式欢迎会。

examples.english: The pair were spotted together at a champagne reception at the Imperial Hotel.
examples.chinese: 他俩在帝国酒店举行的香槟酒会上双双被人认出。

examples.english: Two hundred guests attended an evening reception, held in honor of the Chancellor's visit.
examples.chinese: 二百位宾客出席了欢迎总理来访的晚会。

examples.english: The wedding reception will take place at the Lennox Hotel, starting at 3.30 pm.
examples.chinese: 婚宴将于下午3点半在伦诺克斯酒店举行。

centers.english: to have a party

examples.english: We used to have a big Christmas party every year.
examples.chinese: 我们过去每年举办大型圣诞晚会。

examples.english: The couple had their wedding reception at the Museum of Modern Art.
examples.chinese: 那对夫妇在现代艺术博物馆举办了婚宴。

examples.english: Mark, my boss, had a surprise party to welcome me home.
examples.chinese: 我的老板马克举办一场惊喜聚会欢迎我回来。

examples.english: He threw a huge party to celebrate making his first million dollars.
examples.chinese: 他举办盛大宴会，庆祝自己赚得首个一百万。

examples.english: The staff and patients threw a going-away party for Dr Rogers.
examples.chinese: 医护人员和病人为罗杰斯医生举行欢送会。

examples.english: The Krugers threw an impromptu cocktail party for him in the backyard.
examples.chinese: 克鲁格一家在后院为他临时举行鸡尾酒会。

examples.english: The anniversary dinner was held Wednesday night at the Washington Hilton.
examples.chinese: 周年庆典的宴会星期三晚上在华盛顿的希尔顿酒店举行。

examples.english: They are holding a fund-raising reception on Friday in the City Hall.
examples.chinese: 星期五，他们要在市政厅举行筹款招待会。

expressions.text: give a party/dinner party/lunch party etc

examples.english: I'm giving a dinner party on Thursday night. Would you like to come?
examples.chinese: 星期四晚上我举行晚宴，你想来吗?

examples.english: On Maggie's last night in the house, Jo gave a little farewell party.
examples.chinese: 玛吉在家的最后一晚，乔举办了一个小型告别会。

examples.english: The prospect of giving a dinner party for my boyfriend's snobbish parents filled me with gloom.
examples.chinese: 想到要为男友势利的父母举办晚宴我就心情沮丧。

examples.english: The last show of the tour was in Atlanta, and I decided to give a party for the singers and musicians.
examples.chinese: 巡回演出最后一场在亚特兰大进行，我决定为歌手和乐手举行一场晚会。

examples.english: Colette will be hosting a cocktail reception at 6.00 pm in the Grosvenor Suite.
examples.chinese: 晚上6点科利特将在格罗夫纳套房举行鸡尾酒招待会。

examples.english: Last year, the city hosted a three-day gay pride festival.
examples.chinese: 去年，该市举行了为期三天的同性恋骄傲节。

examples.english: I meet a lot of people, but I don't entertain much myself.
examples.chinese: 我交游很广，但自己设宴招待可不多。

examples.english: Being a sociable person, Eva loved entertaining.
examples.chinese: 伊娃热衷于交际，她喜欢办宴会招待客人。

examples.english: An important part of the job is entertaining business clients.
examples.chinese: 这个职位的一部分重要工作是款待客户。

centers.english: to go to a party

examples.english: Are you going to the Christmas party?
examples.chinese: 你打算参加圣诞晚会吗？

examples.english: Over 150 people went to her 21st birthday party.
examples.chinese: 一百五十多人参加了她的21岁生日聚会。

examples.english: The Duchess of York attended the charity reception, along with her two daughters.
examples.chinese: 约克公爵夫人同两个女儿一起出席了慈善招待会。

examples.english: Some of the most glittering celebrities in the country are expected to attend.
examples.chinese: 该国一些最光彩夺目的名人有望出席。

examples.english: They partied till 7 in the morning.
examples.chinese: 他们在聚会上一直闹到早上7点。

examples.english: I could party all night long!
examples.chinese: 我可以在聚会上玩一整夜！

examples.english: My life was an endless round of photo shoots, interviews, and serious partying.
examples.chinese: 我的生活是拍照、接受采访和在聚会中尽情玩乐，如此循环不已。

examples.english: Partying and having a good time was all she seemed interested in.
examples.chinese: 赴宴和玩乐似乎是她所有的兴趣所在。

examples.english: Around about 2 am, the partying came to an abrupt halt.
examples.chinese: 大约在凌晨2点左右，聚会突然停止了。

centers.english: the people at a party

examples.english: I was intrigued to learn that our Chinese host had spent many years in Chicago.
examples.chinese: 得知招待我们的中国人在芝加哥居住了多年激起了我的好奇。

examples.english: Pam, you've been a great hostess - thank you.
examples.chinese: 帕姆，你真是个了不起的女主人—谢谢你。

examples.english: Lily poured her guest a glass of sherry.
examples.chinese: 莉莉给客人倒了一杯雪利酒。

examples.english: After the wedding, the couple staged a huge reception for over 250 guests.
examples.chinese: 婚礼过后，夫妇俩为250多人举办了盛大的宴会。

examples.english: This year's guest of honour will be the novelist Margaret Attwood.
examples.chinese: 今年的贵宾是小说家玛格丽特·阿特伍德。

examples.english: Lee says the damage was caused by a couple of unknown gatecrashers.
examples.chinese: 李称破坏是一些不请自到的陌生宾客造成的。

centers.english: a country

examples.english: Brazil is one of the biggest countries in the world.
examples.chinese: 巴西是世界上最大的国家之一。

examples.english: The northeast of the country will experience heavy rainfall and high winds.
examples.chinese: 该国东北部地区将有暴雨，并刮大风。

examples.english: Most people in this country are worried about the economy.
examples.chinese: 此国大多数人都在为经济担心。

examples.english: Riots and demonstrations broke out all over the country after the assassination of Martin Luther King.
examples.chinese: 马丁·路德·金遭暗杀后，全国各地都爆发了骚乱和示威游行。

examples.english: Japan has become one of the richest nations in the world.
examples.chinese: 日本已成为世界上最富裕的国家之一。

examples.english: Representatives from the world's leading industrial nations will meet in Geneva.
examples.chinese: 来自世界上各主要工业国的代表将在日内瓦举行会议。

examples.english: We are a nation of both great wealth and terrible poverty.
examples.chinese: 我们是个集巨大财富和极度贫困于一身的国家。

examples.english: In 1830, Greece became an independent state.
examples.chinese: 1830年，希腊成为独立国家。

examples.english: The state of Israel was created in 1948.
examples.chinese: 以色列国建于1948年。

examples.english: For more than 70 years, the former Soviet Union was a one-party state.
examples.chinese: 七十多年来，苏联一直是一党执政的国家。

examples.english: the member states of the European Union
examples.chinese: 欧盟各成员国

examples.english: The western powers hardly knew how to react to this threat.
examples.chinese: 西方各大强国几乎不知道如何应对这种威胁。

examples.english: France was the only European power not to sign the treaty.
examples.chinese: 法国是唯一拒绝签署该条约的欧洲大国。

examples.english: Germany's strong industrial base has helped maintain its status as a major world power.
examples.chinese: 德国强大的工业基础有助于保持其世界主要强国的地位。

examples.english: The book traces the emergence of China as a superpower in the 21st century.
examples.chinese: 该书探讨了21世纪中国崛起为超级大国的前因后果。

examples.english: His travels in foreign lands provided him with the inspiration for many of his poems and songs.
examples.chinese: 他在外国的游历为他提供了许多诗与歌曲的灵感。

examples.english: Our story takes place in a far-off land, long, long ago.
examples.chinese: 我们的故事发生在很久很久以前的一个遥远的国度。

centers.english: a country that is partly or completely controlled by another
centers.chinese: 局部或全部被另一国家控制的国家

examples.english: The United States was once a colony of Great Britain.
examples.chinese: 美国以前曾是英国的殖民地。

examples.english: In 1980, the former British colony of Rhodesia gained independence as the Republic of Zimbabwe.
examples.chinese: 1980年，原为英国殖民地的罗得西亚获得独立，成为津巴布韦共和国。

examples.english: The people of Africa have successfully fought against colonial rule.
examples.chinese: 非洲人民反抗殖民统治的斗争已取得成效。

centers.english: land that belongs to a particular country

examples.english: Colombian guerrillas had reportedly been operating in Venezuelan territory.
examples.chinese: 据报道，哥伦比亚游击队一直在委内瑞拉领土上活动。

examples.english: Ecevit campaigned in May 1991 to have foreign troops removed from Turkish territory.
examples.chinese: 1991年5月，埃杰维特发起运动要求外国军队撤出土耳其领土。

examples.english: The treaty will be signed on US soil.
examples.chinese: 该条约将在美国国土上签署。

examples.english: This was the first time that the Pope had set foot on Cuban soil.
examples.chinese: 这是教皇首次踏上古巴国土。

centers.english: all the people who live in a particular country

examples.english: It was a fair election, the people have made their decision, and we must all accept it.
examples.chinese: 这是一场公平的竞选，人民已经作了决定，我们必须接受他们的判断。

examples.english: UK Prime Minister Tony Blair said the British should stand shoulder to shoulder with the American people.
examples.chinese: 英国首相布莱尔说英国人民应当同美国人民肩并肩站在一起。

examples.english: ‘Millions of our people are still victims of poverty,’ said Mandela.
examples.chinese: “数百万人民仍旧在受贫穷之苦。”曼德拉说。

examples.english: To what extent did the people of Italy support the new government?
examples.chinese: 意大利国民支持新政府到何种程度？

examples.english: The President will make a radio broadcast to the nation this evening.
examples.chinese: 总统将于今晚向全国人民发表广播演说。

examples.english: When Churchill died, the whole nation went into mourning.
examples.chinese: 丘吉尔逝世，举国哀悼。

examples.english: The president seems to no longer care what the country thinks.
examples.chinese: 总统好像已不介意人民的想法。

examples.english: People think our country has lost its sense of purpose and direction.
examples.chinese: 民众认为我们国家已经失去了目标感和方向感。

examples.english: In many Western European countries the population is no longer increasing.
examples.chinese: 在许多西欧国家，人口已停止增长。

examples.english: The population of Germany is about 80 million.
examples.chinese: 德国人口大约有8,000万。

examples.english: The country's Jewish population was angered by the prime minister's remarks.
examples.chinese: 该国的犹太人被总理的言辞激怒了。

centers.english: someone from a particular country

examples.english: Noriko's a Japanese citizen, but her parents are originally from South Korea.
examples.chinese: 典子是日本公民，但她的父母原籍韩国。

examples.english: Fahd became a British citizen after living there for several years.
examples.chinese: 法德在英国居住几年后成为英国公民。

examples.english: Claire is now a citizen of the US.
examples.chinese: 克莱尔现在是美国公民。

examples.english: There are many Russian nationals living in Frankfurt.
examples.chinese: 法兰克福居住着许多俄国侨民。

examples.english: During the war, foreign nationals were forced to leave the country.
examples.chinese: 战争期间，外国侨民被迫离开该国。

examples.english: Picasso was a native of Spain, although he spent much of his life in France.
examples.chinese: 毕加索是西班牙人，虽然他大半生是在法国度过的。

centers.english: the legal right to live in a particular country
centers.chinese: 居住在某个国家的合法权利

examples.english: The application form asks you to state your name, age, and nationality.
examples.chinese: 申请表要求填上姓名、年龄和国籍。

examples.english: Despite being born in Germany, these children do not have an automatic right to German nationality.
examples.chinese: 尽管这些孩子在德国出生，他们并不能自动获得德国国籍。

examples.english: Jeanne has dual nationality because her mother is French and her father is English.
examples.chinese: 珍妮具有双重国籍，因为她母亲是法国人，父亲是英国人。

examples.english: The nationalities of the plane crash victims have not yet been released.
examples.chinese: 飞机失事罹难者的国籍尚未公布。

examples.english: After five years in the US, foreign nationals can apply for full US citizenship.
examples.chinese: 在美国居住五年后，外国侨民可以申请美国公民身份。

examples.english: Krebs was granted French citizenship in 1992.
examples.chinese: 1992年，克雷布斯正式取得法国公民身份。

centers.english: the country where you were born

examples.english: Many of the refugees are keen to return to their home countries now that the fighting has stopped.
examples.chinese: 战争结束，许多难民渴望回到祖国。

examples.english: Jesper Parnevik became the first Swedish golfer to win a European Tour event in his home country.
examples.chinese: 杰斯珀·帕内维克是首位在瑞典赢得欧洲巡回赛的瑞典高尔夫选手。

examples.english: After spending seven years in Japan, Claude returned to his home country of France.
examples.chinese: 在日本住了七年后，克劳德回到了祖国法国。

examples.english: Connery is a nationalist and fiercely proud of his native land.
examples.chinese: 康纳里是位民族主义者，极以自己的祖国为傲。

examples.english: Thousands of Irish families left their native land and went to America in search of a better life.
examples.chinese: 千千万万爱尔兰家庭背井离乡，前往美国寻求更好的生活。

centers.english: having a great love of your country

examples.english: At Llewellyn's funeral service, she was remembered as a patriotic American who had served her country well.
examples.chinese: 在卢埃林的葬礼上，她被人视为为国尽忠的爱国的美国人而受到缅怀。

examples.english: Voting is part of your patriotic duty.
examples.chinese: 投票是爱国之心的部分表现。

examples.english: De Gaulle will always be remembered as a great French patriot.
examples.chinese: 作为法国一位伟大的爱国者，戴高乐将永远被人们所怀念。

examples.english: One of McCarthy's most evil tactics was to question the patriotism of his opponents.
examples.chinese: 麦卡锡最恶毒的策略之一就是对反对者的爱国心表示怀疑。

examples.english: The senator's strong nationalistic views are frightening to many liberals.
examples.chinese: 那位参议员强烈的民族主义观点在许多自由派看来很可怕。

examples.english: As nationalistic feelings grew, life became increasingly difficult for immigrants.
examples.chinese: 随着民族主义情绪的高涨，移民的生活越来越艰难了。

centers.english: people who want their country or area to be independent
centers.chinese: 希望本国或本地区独立的人

examples.english: His father was a nationalist during the civil war.
examples.chinese: 内战时期，他的父亲是民族主义者。

examples.english: Indian nationalists finally won independence for their country in 1947.
examples.chinese: 1947年，印度民族主义者终于为他们的国家赢得了独立。

examples.english: The Scottish Nationalist Party wants Scotland to be independent of the UK.
examples.chinese: 苏格兰民族党希望苏格兰脱离英国独立。

examples.english: Nationalist candidates managed to win only one seat in the elections.
examples.chinese: 民族主义候选人仅在大选中获得一个席位。

examples.english: The newspaper has campaigned vigorously for the nationalist cause.
examples.chinese: 该报大力鼓吹民族主义。

examples.english: The end of the Cold War was followed by an increase in nationalism in Eastern Europe.
examples.chinese: 冷战结束后，民族主义在东欧兴起。

centers.english: happening in, or relating to a particular country
centers.chinese: 在某国发生的，或与某国有关的

examples.english: The national news comes on at 18:30.
examples.chinese: 18点30分播报国内新闻。

examples.english: Winners of the regional competitions compete in the national finals.
examples.chinese: 地区比赛的优胜者参加全国决赛的角逐。

examples.english: The President's speech covered a range of foreign and domestic issues.
examples.chinese: 总统的演讲涵盖了一系列国内外事件。

examples.english: Volkswagen produce cars both for the domestic market and for export.
examples.chinese: 大众公司生产的汽车供应国内市场和出口。

examples.english: Security on domestic flights in the US has been stepped up considerably.
examples.chinese: 美国国内航班上的安全措施已显著加强。

examples.english: Each country has the right to control its own internal affairs.
examples.chinese: 每个国家都有自己处理内政的权利。

examples.english: Western countries have been accused of interfering in Brazil's internal problems.
examples.chinese: 有人指责西方国家干预巴西国内问题。

examples.english: Sales of electrical goods have increased, both at home and abroad.
examples.chinese: 国内外电器的销量都增加了。

examples.english: Shocked by the events at home, the president cut short his trip to Europe.
examples.chinese: 总统对国内发生的事感到震惊，中止了欧洲之行。

centers.english: belonging to, provided by, or involving a particular country
centers.chinese: 属于某国、由某国提供或涉及某国的

examples.english: Alitalia is the national airline of Italy.
examples.chinese: 意大利航空公司是意大利的国家航空公司。

examples.english: a national policy for energy
examples.chinese: 国家的能源政策

examples.english: the National Museum of Film and Photography in Bradford
examples.chinese: 位于布雷德福德的国家电影与摄影博物馆

examples.english: Medicaid is a large federal health care program for the poor.
examples.chinese: 医疗补助制度是一种帮助穷人的大型联邦保健计划。

examples.english: Several federal officials have been charged with corruption.
examples.chinese: 几名联邦官员被控受贿。
